description: Derive each relational document-reference group's requiredness from the owning materialized scope in jsonSchemaForInsert instead of from raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired.
acceptance-criteria: The relational extraction path populates DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired from jsonSchemaForInsert-derived effective requiredness, not from documentPathsMapping.isRequired.

The requiredness traversal starts at the owning materialized table scope for root, child collection, nested collection, root extension, collection-aligned extension, and extension child collection references.
A reference group is required only when every object segment from the owning scope to the reference object requires the next property and no traversed schema segment is x-nullable.
Optional array and _ext ancestors that only decide whether a row exists do not make columns nullable inside an already materialized collection or extension row.
The derived group requiredness is applied uniformly to the reference group's DocumentId column and every propagated identity or descriptor binding column produced for that reference.
Raw Core.ApiSchema document path requiredness remains unchanged for non-relational consumers; raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired is not authoritative for relational column nullability.
The implementation is schema-driven and contains no SurveyResponse-specific, choice-name-specific, or column-name-substring rule.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/DocumentReferenceMappingsExtractor.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/DeriveColumnsAndBindDescriptorEdgesStep.cs, src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ExtensionTableDerivationPass.cs, and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/SetPasses/ReferenceBindingPass.cs to confirm relational reference nullability follows jsonSchemaForInsert semantics.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests|FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionTableDerivationTests"

description: Update identity-reference validation so identity-component references are checked against jsonSchemaForInsert-derived requiredness and fail fast when an identity path depends on an optional reference group.
acceptance-criteria: The identity-reference guard consumes the derived DocumentReferenceMapping.IsRequired value instead of raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired.
Compilation fails when any identityJsonPaths entry is sourced from a reference object that is not effectively required by jsonSchemaForInsert.
The failure diagnostic names the owning resource, reference object path, and identity path that made the optional identity reference invalid.
Required identity-component references that are required by jsonSchemaForInsert continue to compile and produce NOT NULL reference group columns.
The guard applies uniformly to references in root, collection, nested, extension, and extension child collection scopes.
steps-to-verify: Review identity-reference validation in src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/Build/Steps/ExtractInputs/DocumentReferenceMappingsExtractor.cs and any helper introduced for effective reference requiredness.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests"

description: Add focused relational model unit coverage for jsonSchemaForInsert-derived reference requiredness across required, optional, x-nullable, collection, nested, and extension scopes.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover an optional choice-like reference where documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true but jsonSchemaForInsert does not require the reference object.
Unit tests assert the reference group's DocumentId and propagated identity columns are nullable when the derived reference group is optional.
Unit tests assert required references remain non-nullable when jsonSchemaForInsert requires the reference object.
Unit tests assert x-nullable on a traversed reference object path makes the reference group optional even when documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true.
Unit tests cover child collection and nested collection scopes where row-existence ancestors are ignored inside the materialized row.
Unit tests cover root extension, collection-aligned extension, and extension child collection scopes where optional _ext row-presence ancestors are ignored inside the materialized row.
Unit tests cover the optional identity-reference failure path and assert the diagnostic includes the owning resource, reference path, and identity path.
Constraint or binding tests confirm optional reference groups still receive their per-reference all-or-none CHECK constraints and composite FKs.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~ExtractInputsStepTests|FullyQualifiedName~ReferenceBindingRelationalModelSetPassTests|FullyQualifiedName~ExtensionTableDerivationTests|FullyQualifiedName~ConstraintDerivationRelationalModelSetPassTests"
Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.Tests.Unit

description: Add an authoritative DS52+TPDM generated fixture and wire it into the DDL golden and API-level integration fixture harnesses.
acceptance-criteria: A checked-in authoritative DS52+TPDM fixture directory exists under src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative using a clear name such as ds-5.2-tpdm; the existing ds-5.2 core-only and sample-extension fixtures are not treated as equivalent unless TPDM is actually part of their fixture.json.
The fixture.json loads the real DS 5.2 core ApiSchema plus the real TPDM ApiSchema input, declares both pgsql and mssql dialects, and emits a DDL manifest.
The fixture input source does not depend on bin output or a hand-written SurveyResponse-only schema shortcut.

Inspection of the fixture's SurveyResponse resource schema verifies the responder-choice document reference mappings reproduce the DMS-1206 mismatch: raw documentPathsMapping.isRequired is true while jsonSchemaForInsert does not require any responder choice branch.
The DDL golden test suite includes a named DS52+TPDM authoritative fixture so both PostgreSQL and SQL Server outputs are generated and compared.

The API-level integration harness exposes a FixtureKey such as AuthoritativeDs52Tpdm that resolves to the DS52+TPDM fixture, FixtureContext grants access to SurveyResponse and the prerequisite resources used by the SurveyResponse scenarios, and the integration test README fixture map is updated.
steps-to-verify: Inspect src/dms/backend/Fixtures/authoritative/ds-5.2-tpdm/fixture.json, or the selected DS52+TPDM fixture directory, and its inputs to confirm the fixture is real, checked in, includes TPDM, and declares pgsql and mssql.
Inspect the SurveyResponse resourceSchema in the DS52+TPDM fixture input to confirm the DMS-1206 requiredness mismatch is present.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"

Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Fixtures/FixtureContext.cs, src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Fixtures/FixtureRepositoryPaths.cs, and src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/README.md for the new fixture key mapping.

description: Update the DS52+TPDM authoritative relational model and DDL goldens so SurveyResponse responder-choice reference groups are nullable in both PostgreSQL and SQL Server outputs.
acceptance-criteria: The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected relational-model.pgsql.manifest.json marks the SurveyResponderChoiceContact_*, SurveyResponderChoiceStaff_*, and SurveyResponderChoiceStudent_* reference group columns nullable on edfi.SurveyResponse.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected relational-model.mssql.manifest.json marks the same SurveyResponse reference group columns nullable.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected pgsql.sql emits those SurveyResponse columns without NOT NULL.
The DS52+TPDM fixture's expected mssql.sql emits those SurveyResponse columns without NOT NULL.
The existing per-reference all-or-none CHECK constraints remain present for the contact, staff, and student responder-choice reference groups.
The existing composite FKs remain present for the contact, staff, and student responder-choice reference groups.
No at-most-one or exactly-one choice CHECK constraint is added for SurveyResponse responder choices.
Related effective-schema and DDL manifests are updated when the generated fixture hash, statement counts, or inventories change.
steps-to-verify: Run UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Ddl.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"

Inspect the DS52+TPDM expected relational-model.pgsql.manifest.json, relational-model.mssql.manifest.json, pgsql.sql, and mssql.sql SurveyResponse sections for nullable choice-reference columns, unchanged all-or-none CHECKs, unchanged composite FKs, and absence of choice-group CHECKs.

description: Add API-level relational integration tests against the DS52+TPDM generated DDL for SurveyResponse POST payloads that exercise responder-choice optionality.
acceptance-criteria: A shared API-level scenario posts a SurveyResponse with only the contact responder choice branch and receives a successful create response.
A shared API-level scenario posts a SurveyResponse with only the staff responder choice branch and receives a successful create response.
A shared API-level scenario posts a SurveyResponse with only the student responder choice branch and receives a successful create response.
A shared API-level scenario posts a SurveyResponse with no responder choice branch and receives a successful create response.
PostgreSQL and SQL Server test wrappers bind the shared scenario to the DS52+TPDM FixtureKey; SQL Server may skip when ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin is not configured.
Each scenario creates or seeds the minimum referenced Survey, Contact, Staff, Student, descriptor, and calendar/session prerequisites needed for reference and descriptor validity.
The tests assert the API path does not return the unrecognized relational write failure and, where practical, assert the absent responder-choice branch columns persist as NULL.
The tests do not depend on test order and use unique payload identity values per case.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=PostgresqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~SurveyResponse"

When SQL Server is configured, run dotnet test src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "Category=MssqlIntegration&FullyQualifiedName~SurveyResponse"

Review src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.Integration/Scenarios and the PostgreSQL/MSSQL test wrapper directories to confirm the SurveyResponse scenario uses FixtureKey.AuthoritativeDs52Tpdm or the selected DS52+TPDM fixture key.

description: Run cross-dialect generated DDL smoke and compatibility coverage for the DS52+TPDM fixture after the nullable reference model and goldens are updated.
acceptance-criteria: PostgreSQL generated DDL smoke or compatibility tests apply the DS52+TPDM authoritative DDL successfully.
SQL Server generated DDL smoke or compatibility tests apply the DS52+TPDM authoritative DDL successfully when ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin points at a running SQL Server.
The DS52+TPDM generated DDL records dms.EffectiveSchema correctly and passes the same compatibility-gate expectations used by existing authoritative fixtures.
No DDL smoke failure is introduced by nullable SurveyResponse choice-reference columns, all-or-none CHECKs, or composite FKs.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"
Verify SQL Server is listening and ConnectionStrings__MssqlAdmin is set, or start a temporary SQL Server container as described in AGENTS.md.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Authoritative"

description: Verify the Grand Bend populated bulk load on a DS52+TPDM relational stack after the focused model, DDL, and API integration signals pass.
acceptance-criteria: The dms-local stack is started with the relational backend and DS52+TPDM schema configuration.
The full Grand Bend populated template load completes without SurveyResponse 500 errors.
All SurveyResponse rows from the Grand Bend load are inserted on the relational backend.
Docker logs do not contain NOT NULL violations for SurveyResponderChoiceContact_*, SurveyResponderChoiceStaff_*, or SurveyResponderChoiceStudent_* columns.
steps-to-verify: Run ./build-dms.ps1 E2ETest -Configuration Release -SkipDockerBuild -IdentityProvider self-contained -EnvironmentFile './.env.e2e.relational' -TestFilter 'Category=@relational-backend' to provision and validate the relational stack.
Run pwsh eng/bulkLoad/Invoke-LoadGrandBend.ps1 -BaseUrl 'http://localhost:8080' -FullDataSet with the DS52+TPDM relational DMS endpoint and appropriate key/secret values.
Inspect docker logs for dms-local-dms-1 and the PostgreSQL container for SurveyResponse failures.
Query the relational database to confirm SurveyResponse rows were inserted and the responder choice nullable columns are allowed to be NULL when their branch is absent.
